title: Google & Kaggle AI Agents Course Hub
overview: Welcome to the consolidated AI Agents Course Hub. This interactive dashboard combines the complete curriculum from the foundational course (Dec 2025 Foundation) and the advanced vibe coding updates (Jun 2026 Advanced). Here, you will study how agents compress traditional software cycles, utilize open standards like Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agent-to-Agent (A2A), optimize tokens using modular Skills directories, implement ephemeral sandboxes and evaluations, and scale fleets of governed agents with Spec-Driven Development (SDD) on Google Cloud.

prereqs.title: Kaggle Phone Verification
prereqs.desc: Required to enable notebook environments for Dec 2025 assignments.

prereqs.title: Google AI Studio API Key
prereqs.desc: Generate a developer API key to connect models inside the ADK.

prereqs.title: Antigravity SDK / CLI / IDE
prereqs.desc: Download and set up the local developer suite.

links.title: NotebookLM Study Guide
links.desc: Upload the whitepapers to create an interactive study guide.

links.title: Codelabs Troubleshooting FAQ
links.desc: Review solutions for common notebook error logs.

links.title: Kaggle Discussion Forum
links.desc: Ask questions and share tips with other learners.

links.title: Course Feedback Form
links.desc: Share your experience with the development team.

syllabus.title: Intro & Vibe Coding
syllabus.headerTitle: Unit 1: Introduction to Agents & Vibe Coding
syllabus.intro: Explore the shift from traditional chatbots to autonomous agents. Learn how agentic architectures compress traditional software development cycles (SDLC), and study how vibe coding workflows allow natural language to act as the primary programming interface.
bullets: Foundational Taxonomy: Map the spectrum of agentic capabilities, operational models, and constrained policies.
Vibe Coding Workflows: Transition from manual syntax typing to intent-driven programming with Antigravity 2.0.
Cloud Deployment: Deploy code directly from Google AI Studio to Cloud Run endpoints.

livestream.desc: Deep dive walkthrough of Vibe Coding setups, Gherkin specifications, and workspace deployment options.

syllabus.title: Tools & MCP
syllabus.headerTitle: Unit 2: Agent Tools & Model Context Protocol (MCP)
syllabus.intro: Connect models to databases and APIs safely using unified communication standards. Explore function declaration best practices and understand how Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes integrations without custom wrappers.
bullets: Tools design: Write highly descriptive schemas that allow LLMs to determine arguments reliably.
Model Context Protocol: Connect model runtimes to SQLite databases and local filesystems via stdio host servers.
Long-Running Operations: Design approval checkpoints where agents pause execution until cleared by a human.

podcast.desc: Exploring custom tool calls, API interactions, and early implementations of the Model Context Protocol.

livestream.title: Day 2 Q&A Livestream Panel
livestream.desc: Discussion on tool declarations and long-running operations. Panelists: Edward Grefenstette, Mike Styer, Oriol Vinyals, Alex Wissner-Gross, Laxmi Harikumar.

podcast.title: Tools & Interoperability Podcast
podcast.desc: Diving into Agent-to-Agent links, Universal Commerce, and Generative User Interfaces (A2UI).

pdf.title: Agent Tools & Interoperability

livestream.title: Unit 2 Tools & Interoperability Livestream
livestream.desc: Technical walkthrough of MCP host topologies, inter-agent workflows, and Generative UI widgets.

syllabus.title: Memory & Skills
syllabus.headerTitle: Unit 3: Context Engineering, Sessions, Memory & Skills
syllabus.intro: Optimize token budgets and context windows. Master dynamic context assembly using short-term conversation sessions, persistent vector memory banks, and modular Skills directories to load files only when needed.
bullets: Short-term Sessions: Contain conversation logs in isolated stateful structures to preserve immediate history.
Persistent Memory: Implement external database storage to recall facts across disconnected runs.
Agent Skills: Package code guidelines and prompt instructions in modular directories to prevent token blowup.

podcast.desc: Understanding state management, dynamic context assembly, and short-term vs long-term storage.

pdf.desc: Techniques for token engineering, session container state management, and memory stores.

livestream.title: Day 3 Q&A Livestream Panel
livestream.desc: Discussion on context limits, RAG integration, and long-term memory. Panelists: Steven Johnson, Kimberly Milam, Julia Wiesinger, Jay Alammar, Kristopher Overholt.

podcast.title: Agent Skills Podcast

pdf.title: Agent Skills

livestream.title: Unit 3 Agent Skills Livestream

syllabus.title: Quality & Security
syllabus.headerTitle: Unit 4: Agent Quality, Observability, Security & Evaluation
syllabus.intro: Assure reliability in non-deterministic systems. Implement three-pillar observability (Logs, Traces, Metrics), automate evaluations with LLM-as-a-Judge, and establish Effective Trust boundaries using sandboxing and threat testing.
bullets: Observability: Trace execution trajectories, construct timeline logs, and measure token metrics via OpenTelemetry.
Automated Evaluation: Grade agent tool paths and response qualities using labeled datasets.
Effective Trust: Enforce zero-trust security constraints, sandboxed environments, and package lock validations.

podcast.title: Agent Quality & Evaluation Podcast
podcast.desc: Understanding automated testing, logs vs traces vs metrics, and benchmark evaluation runs.

pdf.title: Agent Quality & Evaluation
pdf.desc: Holistic evaluation strategies, OpenTelemetry pipelines, and building loop feedback flywheels.

livestream.title: Day 4 Q&A Livestream Panel
livestream.desc: Insights on debugging non-deterministic agent loops and LLM judging. Panelists: Wafae Bakkali, Turan Bulmus, Sian Gooding, Jiwei Liu, Sita Lakshmi Sangameswaran.

podcast.title: Security & Evaluation Podcast
podcast.desc: Deep dive on sandboxing untrusted code, slopsquatting defenses, and ambient human review gating.

pdf.title: Vibe Coding Agent Security and Evaluation
pdf.desc: Analysis of continuous verification, the Red/Blue/Green security triad, and sandboxed runtimes.

livestream.title: Unit 4 Security & Evaluation Livestream
livestream.desc: Walkthrough of package scanners, setting permission walls, and setting up telemetry monitors.

syllabus.title: Production & Scale
syllabus.headerTitle: Unit 5: Production deployments, Agent fleets, A2A & Spec-Driven Development
syllabus.intro: Graduate prototypes to managed cloud clusters. Explore Agent-to-Agent (A2A) microservices, deploy runtimes to Google Cloud, and adopt Spec-Driven Development (SDD) to treat individual codebases as completely disposable.
bullets: Agent-to-Agent (A2A): Connect specialized agents across secure networks, bypassing monolithic architectures.
Spec-Driven Development: Treat BDD Gherkin files as the absolute source of truth, generating code dynamically.
Production Scaling: Deploy to Vertex AI Agent Engine or Cloud Run with Policy Server governance.

highlights.title: 5 Core Parts of an Agent
items: 1. The Model: LLM reasoning core.
2. Tools: APIs, function executors.
3. Memory: Short-term sessions & long-term facts.
4. Orchestration: Loops driving agent actions.
5. Deployment: hosting & cloud scales.

highlights.title: 6 Key Types of Context
items: 1. Instructions: Core goals & boundaries.
2. Knowledge: Domain data, retrieved documents.
3. Memory: Local session history logs.
4. Examples: Few-shot templates of output formatting.
5. Tools: Precise JSON schemas of callable APIs.
6. Guardrails: Hard rules & validation checks.

highlights.title: 3 Tiers of Code Review
items: Tier 1 - Managed: Standard off-the-shelf SaaS reviewers (e.g. Gemini Code Assist on GitHub).
Tier 2 - Hybrid: Git Actions executing coding CLI agents locally (e.g. Antigravity CLI).
Tier 3 - Custom: Specialized ADK multi-agent pipelines hosted on managed runtimes.

highlights.title: 3 Policy Gating Steps
items: 1. Structural Check: Fast, binary environment YAML checks (e.g., block tool role access).
2. Semantic Check: Secondary LLM evaluating argument intent (safeguarding unmasked PII).
3. Execution: Run the tool, or fail/trigger self-correction dynamically.

highlights.title: 3 Durable SDD Principles
items: 1. Structure scales, vibes don't: Verification, strict design specs, and test sets are mandatory.
2. AI amplifies code culture: Good engineering teams build harnesses; bad teams generate technical debt.
3. The human role is evolving: Transition from syntax developers to context architects.

highlights.title: 3 Graph Retrieval Modes
items: 1. Graph Traversal (GQL): Query structures (find every function that transitively calls target API).
2. Vector Search (ANN): Semantic query lookup.
3. Full-Text Search: Exact identifier matches.

highlights.title: 3 Observability Pillars
items: 1. Logs (The Diary): Detailed debug execution history.
2. Traces (The Narrative): Visual call graphs and step sequences.
3. Metrics (The Health Report): Cost, latency, and tokens usage.

highlights.title: 2 Types of Memory
items: Short-term (Sessions): Context contained in a single conversation history.
Long-term (Memory Banks): Persistent facts recalled across different chat sessions.

highlights.title: 2 Integration Protocols
items: Model Context Protocol (MCP): For agent-to-tool connections.
Agent-to-Agent (A2A): For agent-to-agent remote delegation.

highlights.title: 2 Evaluation Pillars
items: Output Evaluation: Compares output against a correct baseline.
Trajectory Evaluation: Scans the sequence of tools called and reasoning paths.

glossary.definition: A design pattern focusing on autonomous loop execution (perceive, plan, act, observe) rather than single-turn LLM chat prompts.
Operations frameworks and disciplines required to monitor, version control, and govern agent logic in production.
Agent Development Kit (ADK) - Google's toolkit for structuring agents, defining tools, and managing session loops.
Gated functions enabling agents to pause executing tool tasks while awaiting manual human checkpoint clearance.
The practice of dynamically assembling high-signal context information inside the model's active window.
The metrics and evaluation frameworks used to score agent success rates, hallucination logs, and output validity.
An evaluation paradigm where a secondary, specialized model scores agent trajectories against pre-labeled rubrics.
A managed hosting platform on Google Cloud designed to deploy, run, and scale ADK agents.
A programming paradigm where developers express requirements in natural language and guide code-writing models to generate the implementation, acting as context architects.
A structured, disciplined methodology surrounding AI outputs with tests, sandboxes, evaluations, and policy restrictions to ensure reliability.
The complete scaffolding wrapped around raw AI models: includes system prompts, external tools, local policies, sandboxes, sub-agents, and observability scripts.
A framework-agnostic standard ('Generative Display Window') that lets agents compositionally arrange pre-approved UI parts (cards, sliders) for safe user interactions.
A protocol laying the groundwork for secure, machine-to-machine financial payments governed by human-approved spending mandates.
A programmatic commerce layer standardizing checkout, shopping cart validation, and merchant catalogs for autonomous buyer agents.
Modular bundles of files and instructions structured around a central SKILL.md document. Dynamically loaded on-demand depending on task matching to prevent token overloading.
The degradation in reasoning ability and increase in errors when an agent's context window is overloaded with noisy or irrelevant data.
An optimization tactic loading full code instructions or documentation fragments only when the active task requires them.
A security vulnerability where attackers publish malicious packages using names hallucinated by coding agents, tricking systems into importing infected libraries.
A zero-trust security architecture enforcing continuous verification, sandboxed subprocess runtimes, and trajectory check gates.
A development process where behavior-driven Gherkin specifications are treated as the project's source of truth, rendering individual codebases disposable.
A hybrid security filter intercepting tool calls. Enforces deterministic Structural Gating (YAML roles checks) and Semantic Gating (LLM PII check).
A regex utility replacing double-bracketed placeholders in templates with environment values, sanitizing inputs.
An open standard protocol ('the USB-C for AI tools') that instantly connects AI models directly to SQLite/Postgres databases, filesystems, and remote APIs.
A standardized communication protocol ('Factory Radio') enabling distributed specialized agents to communicate, negotiate, and delegate tasks to each other.

nodes.def: A design pattern focusing on autonomous loop execution (perceive, plan, act, observe) rather than single-turn LLM chat prompts.
Operations frameworks and disciplines required to monitor, version control, and govern agent logic in production.
Gated functions enabling agents to pause executing tool tasks while awaiting manual human checkpoint clearance.
The practice of dynamically assembling high-signal context information inside the model's active window.
A container managing the current conversational state and active logs of a single user chat.
Long-term vector databases holding rules, configurations, and user facts across different session chats.
takeaways: Avoids starting from a blank slate

nodes.def: The metrics and evaluation frameworks used to score agent success rates, hallucination logs, and output validity.
The trace metrics, debugger log lines, and execution paths providing complete visibility into agent decisions.
An evaluation paradigm where a secondary, specialized model scores agent trajectories against pre-labeled rubrics.
A managed hosting platform on Google Cloud designed to deploy, run, and scale ADK agents.
A programming paradigm where developers express requirements in natural language and guide code-writing models to generate the implementation, acting as context architects.
takeaways: Code is disposable; specifications are the target

nodes.def: A disciplined engineering practice utilizing strict constraints, tests, sandboxes, and verification loops surrounding LLM outputs.
takeaways: Requires upfront CapEx, but drops operational OpEx

nodes.def: The entire scaffolding wrapped around a raw reasoning model that enables it to perform complex, safe, and observable tasks.
takeaways: Includes: sandboxes, tools, prompt rules, observability hooks
Scaffold represents 90% of the engineering work

nodes.def: A secure protocol (A2UI) allowing agents to return structured, dynamic user interfaces composed of pre-approved layout components.
takeaways: Generative UI: Composes interfaces on demand safely
Client renders UI components natively (LEGO blocks)

nodes.def: An open payment protocol (AP2) providing standard transactions and cryptographically signed rules between agents and merchants.
takeaways: Sets secure wallets and credit limits
Mandates govern spending ranges (no hidden fees)

nodes.def: A standard protocol (UCP) translating catalogs, carts, orders, and checkouts machine-readably for autonomous agents.
takeaways: Enables agents to interact directly with merchant inventories
Acts as the pipeline for programmatic supply chain purchases

nodes.def: Portable directories organized around a SKILL.md file containing specialized prompt workflows and scripts.
takeaways: Dynamic context packages loaded only on demand
Reduces system prompts and context bloat
Prevents context rot and attention dilution

nodes.def: An optimization tactic loading full code instructions or documentation fragments only when the active task requires them.
takeaways: Initial state has only minimal metadata
Saves token costs and prevents model distraction
Core mechanism of Google ADK and Agent Skills

nodes.def: The decline of LLM reasoning performance and accuracy when the context window is stuffed with noise or generic files.
takeaways: Caused by dumping complete repositories into prompts
Can lead to severe hallucinations or tool mistakes
Resolved by dynamic routing and RAG for tools

nodes.def: A security posture establishing continuous verification of agent execution, rather than relying on one-time authentication check gates.
takeaways: Enforces sandboxed runtimes and trajectory reviews
Defends against rogue behavior or compromise

nodes.def: A risk vector where attackers publish malicious packages using names hallucinated by coding agents, waiting to be imported.
takeaways: Requires automated safety threat scans in build pipelines
Demands sandbox barriers during local code testing

nodes.def: A workflow (SDD) treating behavior-driven Gherkin scenarios as the source of truth, meaning implementation code is disposable.
takeaways: Scenarios write Given / When / Then specifications
Code can be regenerated completely in JavaScript or Python
Shifts developers from coding to review and design

nodes.def: A hybrid governance server intercepting tool calls to enforce Structural Gating (YAML roles) and Semantic Gating (LLM check).
takeaways: Structural layer: Checks permissions deterministically in YAML
Semantic layer: Secondary LLM reviews arguments for secret leaks
Separate execution logic from governance safety

nodes.def: A regex utility replacing double-bracketed placeholders in templates with environment values, sanitizing inputs.
takeaways: Avoids hardcoding keys or emails in codebase specs

nodes.def: An open standard connecting models to APIs and databases natively without custom REST wrappers.
A standard communication protocol enabling remote, domain-specific specialist agents to coordinate and share conversational state.
